task_completion.description: Verify that the observer location is set to Newgrange with the correct coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium location window or the bottom status bar should display 'Newgrange' or the coordinates approximately 53.69°N, 6.47°W (or -6.47°), and altitude 75m.

task_completion.description: Verify that the required display flags are set: landscape disabled, azimuthal grid visible, and cardinal directions visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium sky view should show the green azimuthal grid lines, cardinal direction letters (N, S, E, W) on the horizon, and the ground/landscape should be disabled (transparent or black below the horizon line).

task_completion.description: Verify the ancient solstice sunrise observation at 3200 BCE.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium date/time in the bottom status bar or time window should read year -3199 (or 3200 BCE), month 12, day 21, at approximately 08:30 UTC. The Sun should be visible and centered near the horizon.

task_completion.description: Verify the modern solstice sunrise observation at 2024 CE.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium date/time in the bottom status bar or time window should read year 2024, month 12, day 21, at approximately 08:30 UTC. The Sun should be visible and centered near the horizon.

task_completion.description: Verify that the research notes were documented correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor should be open showing the file '/home/ga/Desktop/newgrange_alignment_notes.txt'. The text must include the site name ('Newgrange'), mention 'solstice', the ancient date (-3199 or 3200 BCE), and an observed sunrise azimuth direction (a degree value).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used the Stellarium application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the authentic Stellarium graphical user interface, menus, and rendering, not just a web browser or an image viewer showing pre-existing pictures.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually observed the azimuth rather than hallucinating or copying the prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: The notes file should contain a specific numerical degree value for the azimuth direction, indicating the agent actually looked at the Stellarium grid/compass to take a measurement.

integrity.description: Ensure the notes file was created and edited properly.
integrity.visual_evidence: The notes should be visible in a standard GUI text editor (like gedit, mousepad, or nano in a terminal) showing the correct file path.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Location: Newgrange (Lat 53.6947°N, Lon -6.4755°W, Alt 75m). Required Dates: December 21, -3199 (ancient) and December 21, 2024 (modern) at 08:30 UTC. Required Display Flags: Landscape disabled (false), Azimuthal Grid enabled (true), Cardinal Points enabled (true), Atmosphere enabled (true). Expected Outputs: At least 2 screenshots saved in /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/ and a notes file at /home/ga/Desktop/newgrange_alignment_notes.txt containing 'Newgrange', 'solstice', the ancient date, and the observed azimuth.
